body:
**Scope:** the verification campaign — M3's tier mapping is **Tier 0–2; Jepsen consistency; disk-fault injection for scrub/repair** (§13.4); **no Tier 3** (M5). **Tier-0 DST** (the correctness authority, §13.1 — custodians run inside the simulator): reconstruct-to-full-redundancy with no read errors (Q1); commit-point-atomic repair under crash at every pipeline step (never a hybrid; crashed repair = garbage, not corruption); scrub detects bit-rot then reconstructs (Q2); GC reclaims only true orphans, never a referenced fragment, never a torn reader (Q3); a fenced stale leader lands no location update; durability-plane emission validated by **assertion** (under-replicated count rises then returns to zero). **Tier-1:** disk-fault injection (**dm-flakey / dm-error**, FUSE) over the scrub/checksum path + **Jepsen** consistency over repair. **Tier-2:** single real node — kill a real D server, watch real reconstruction over real NVMe/fsync. Bug-finding seeds committed as permanent regressions.

**Definition of done**
- [ ] Tier-0 custodian property suite (Q1–Q3, atomicity-under-crash, fenced-stale-leader, telemetry emission) green in the seed sweep; seeds committed
- [ ] Tier-1 dm-flakey/dm-error scrub + Jepsen consistency green
- [ ] Tier-2 single-node kill-and-reconstruct green in CI
- [ ] every real-world discovery promoted back into DST as a seeded regression

**Needs:** M3.4, M3.5, M3.6, M3.7. **Refs:** proposal 0005 §"DST and tests"; architecture §13, §10 (Q1–Q3); ADR-0009.

milestone.title: M3 — Custodians
milestone.description: The self-maintaining durability plane (L4): GC, scrub, reconstruction, rebalance + durability telemetry. Makes single-zone trustworthy; gates the Step-2 release. Plan: proposal 0005.

title: M3.8 — DST campaign (Tier 0) + Tier-1 disk-fault/Jepsen + Tier-2 kill-and-reconstruct
